verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone retained by KDEL. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells, stressed cells, and cells undergoing immunogenic cell death — where it functions as a co-receptor and is recognized by antibodies, ADCs, and CAR-T constructs directed against the cell surface. This cancer/stress-induced ecto-GRP78 pool is reproducibly detected by flow cytometry and surface biotinylation. The surface display is cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation, ER stress, hypoxia, ICD) rather than constitutive. Baseline localization is ER-luminal, making this contextual/cell_state_induced.
